etymology.description: semantic 玉(玉) (yù, "jade") + phonetic 其(其) (qí, "his") -> fine jade
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 玉(玉) (yù, "jade"), indicating the material or essence of preciousness, with the phonetic 其(其) (qí, "his"), to convey the sound. The specific meaning of 'fine jade' arises from the semantic component representing high-quality and valuable jade.

examples.chinese: 她的名字是琪(她的名字是琪)。
他送了一块美丽的琪玉(他送了一塊美麗的琪玉)。
examples.pinyin: Tā sòng le yī kuài měilì de qí yù.
examples.english: He gave a beautiful jade stone.

examples.chinese: 琪家住在山的另一边(琪家住在山的另一邊)。
examples.pinyin: Qí jiā zhù zài shān de lìng yī biān.
examples.english: The Qi family lives on the other side of the mountain.
